period_focus: 2024 outcome, 2026 official program/pricing, 2026-2028 global growth targets
evidence_level: B+ success benchmark, C marketing-attribution caveat
short_verdict: A strong success benchmark for paid experiential content and local-capture design. The strongest causal candidates are product-market fit, paid mud-experience zones, local voucher return, night/performance programming, foreign visitor appeal and heat/weather operations; contractor success attribution remains unproven.
why_it_matters: It shows a practical alternative to free, high-cost festivals: paid core experience plus local voucher return.
It provides measurable success variables: paid entrants, foreign visitors, stay time, per-person spend and local coupon redemption.
It helps separate real demand from headline attendance by looking at paid conversion and local capture.
It gives a success-side comparator for price fairness because official ticket and food prices are publicly visible.

stakeholders.commercial_nodes: paid mud-experience zones, food vendors, local coupon partners, merchandise/cosmetics
stakeholders.contractor_causality_status: not proven from current public evidence

causal_chain.evidence: KTO lists 2026 dates, paid general/family zone prices and the host/operator structure. The official ticket page details general/family zone prices, discount groups, safety-inspection closure day, online/offline sales mix and 5,000 KRW Boryeong Love gift certificate per ticket.
Yonhap reported 1,651,943 visitors including 82,598 foreigners, despite early monsoon/rain, and 39,905 paid experience-zone entrants.
KTO and Yonhap list K-pop, hip-hop, night parade, drone show, mud water events, exhibitions/sales, local products and global food zones.
2026 coverage states Boryeong aims to lift foreign participant share from 0.74% in 2025 to 3.5% by 2028, revisit from 21.2% to 30% and foreign per-person spend from 237,565 KRW to 300,000 KRW.
A critical 2026 analysis cites stay time of 4h13m and per-person spend of 160,946 KRW, but asks whether results stem from content competitiveness or large promotion spend.

not_supported_claims: The festival succeeds because of a specific contractor.
Visitor count alone proves budget efficiency.
Paid experience-zone entrants alone represent all festival value.
Voucher return automatically proves local merchant benefit without redemption data.
next_evidence_needed: Ticket sales by day and zone
Boryeong Love gift certificate redemption by merchant category
Local Finance 365 cost rows and contract/RFP scope

sources.title: KTO Visit Korea festival page
sources.supports: 2026 period, prices, operator, program mix, local-capture programs and food-price visibility

sources.supports: ticket prices, discounts, safety inspection day, online/offline sales and 5,000 KRW gift certificate
